name: Task UI
responsibility: React components for the primary user-facing views: menubar dashboard panel, Layer 1 task cards, briefing panel, capture bar, and drift/intent editing UI. All components communicate with the Rust backend exclusively through Tauri IPC commands (via App Shell).

interfaces.name: DashboardPanel

output_schema.renders: List of TaskSummary items with status badges and platform color indicators

interfaces.api_detail: Root panel shown when menubar icon is clicked. Renders all tasks as compact rows: name, platform color dots, status badge (running/completed/parked), unreviewed count. Clicking a task row opens Layer 1 card. Provides buttons for: create task, park all, open briefing.

interfaces.name: TaskCard
interfaces.api_detail: Layer 1 card component. Fetches data via ipc:task_get_card. Renders 5 zones: (1) Current intent with version badge and edit button, (2) Agent progress bars grouped by platform, (3) Drift indicator (orange banner if any branch has drift), (4) Resume note (auto or manual), (5) Quick action buttons (open diff, open PR, open terminal). Card has a pull-down handle to expand into Layer 2 graph. Starts review timer on mount (ipc:start_review).

interfaces.name: IntentEditor

output_schema.renders: Inline edit form for intent refinement

interfaces.api_detail: Inline editor within TaskCard. Shows current statement with edit pencil icon. On edit: text input + trigger selector (refinement/drift_response) + optional reason. Submits via ipc:refine_intent. After submit, card refreshes to show new version.

interfaces.name: DriftMarkerUI

output_schema.renders: Drift marking button and summary input

interfaces.api_detail: Button on each agent branch row in the card. Click opens a one-line text input for drift summary. Submits via ipc:mark_drift. After marking drift, prompts user to refine intent (opens IntentEditor with trigger='drift_response').

interfaces.name: BriefingPanel

output_schema.renders: Sorted list of agent events since last briefing

interfaces.api_detail: Fetches briefing via ipc:get_briefing. Renders events grouped by task, sorted by priority (drift first, upstream dependencies first, completed before running). Each event row shows: task name, platform badge, event type icon, summary. Clicking a task row navigates to its TaskCard. Drift-flagged items have orange left border.

interfaces.name: CaptureBar

output_schema.renders: Slim bar at top of panel with auto note and optional text input

interfaces.api_detail: Appears after auto-capture completes (listens to capture.completed event). Shows auto-generated resume note with a text input to override. Auto-dismisses after 3 seconds if user doesn't interact. Dismiss saves auto note; typing saves manual note via ipc:save_resume_note.

interfaces.name: ManualBranchForm

output_schema.renders: Form for creating/updating manual agent branches

interfaces.api_detail: Accessible from TaskCard via '+' button. Fields: platform name (text with suggestions: codex, cursor, custom), platform color (color picker with presets), status, progress (optional slider), output link (optional URL). Submits via ipc:create_manual_branch or ipc:update_agent_branch.

interfaces.name: ReviewOutcomeBar

output_schema.renders: Approve/Reject action bar for completing a review

interfaces.api_detail: Shown at bottom of TaskCard when reviewing a specific branch. Three buttons: Approve, Reject, Reject Partial. Clicking calls ipc:end_review with the outcome. Silently records review duration.

fields.name: platform_colors
fields.constraints: Preset: claude_code=#14B8A6 (teal), codex=#F59E0B (amber), cursor=#3B82F6 (blue). Custom platforms get auto-assigned from palette.

nfr_constraints.performance: TaskCard render < 100ms after data arrives (IPC + render total < 200ms per INV-005). Dashboard list renders up to 8 tasks without virtualization.
nfr_constraints.other: All components are React functional components with hooks. State management via React context (no external state library needed for this scale). Tauri IPC calls wrapped in custom hooks (useTask, useBriefing, useCapture) for reuse.
